attacking_style: Long-range solo attacks from established breakaways, typically launching 20-25km from finish on flat-to-rolling terrain. Prefers attacking from numerical advantage groups rather than initiating breaks alone.
team_tactics: Functions flexibly within Deceuninck system — capable of both breakaway exploitation and leadout duties when primary plans fail. Team allows freedom in intermediate groups for GC opportunism.
terrain_preference: Flat-to-rolling breakaway stages where endurance positioning and multiple accelerations wear down companions. Excels when organizers allow sustained gaps without peloton pursuit.
vulnerability: Relies on disorganized chasing to maintain gaps; vulnerable when peloton commits organized pursuit. Bonus-hunting can compromise breakaway cooperation and reduce win probability.
key_patterns: Attacks from established breakaways rather than initiating moves, launches 20-25km out (2 observations)
Multiple accelerations before decisive move, tests companions' endurance before final attack (2 observations)
Targets intermediate bonuses aggressively, sometimes prioritizing GC seconds over stage cooperation (2 observations)
Versatile team role: primary breakaway attacker and backup leadout capacity documented (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2020 showed peak breakaway execution with win; 2021 relegated to support role suggests tactical reassignment rather than form decline
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 16
